deployment_question: How does the model transfer to a species or taxonomic group absent from training?
definition: Hold out one or more species, strains, clades, or taxonomic groups to evaluate transfer across evolutionary distance.

split_operator.spectra_mapping: sets same-taxon overlap to zero and samples phylogenetic novelty

implementation_recipe: Choose the taxonomic rank matching deployment.
Assign all samples from held-out taxa to test.
Report phylogenetic distances from test taxa to training taxa.
quality_gates: Clarify whether transfer is cross-strain, cross-species, or cross-clade.
Check label distribution and sampling effort by taxon.
Measure taxonomic and sequence-level similarity separately.
failure_modes: Models may exploit taxonomic artifacts rather than the intended biology.
Uneven sampling across taxa can dominate performance.
Ortholog mapping can leak information across species.

citations.title: The Power of Universal Contextualized Protein Embeddings in Cross-species Protein Function Prediction
citations.evidence: Studies transfer of protein function prediction across species.

citations.title: Cross-species modeling of plant genomes at single-nucleotide resolution
citations.evidence: Evaluates genomic language models across plant species after evolutionary divergence.
